factors.name: Semiconductor earnings disappointment
factors.summary: Broadcom set to shed $300B in value as AI results fail to impress; Micron flirts with record market-cap wipeout as Broadcom casts shadow over chip stocks. This is a major NDX headwind given heavy semiconductor weighting.

factors.name: Tech megacap selloff signals
factors.summary: Netflix down 24% since last earnings amid competitive pressures; Apple Services growth concerns noted by UBS; CrowdStrike and Broadcom underperforming expectations. Single-name weakness in large NDX constituents.

factors.name: Fed hawkish inflation messaging
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid calls inflation 'biggest risk' to US economy; persistent inflation narrative with 5-year fight ongoing. Daly confirms inflation remains top priority. This pressures long-duration growth valuations in NDX.

factors.name: Rotation into value/financials
factors.summary: Dow Jones surges 1.84% led by financials (GS +4.93%, JPM +3.64%, AXP +4.41%) — clear rotation OUT of tech into real economy. This suggests near-term NDX underperformance vs. broader market.

factors.name: Geopolitical relief narrative
factors.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, Trump peace deal optimism, Putin signals willingness for Ukraine compromise. De-risking momentum supports equity appetite and lowers safe-haven USD bid, neutral-to-bullish for risk assets.

factors.name: IPO momentum and capital rotation
factors.summary: Quantinuum +13% above IPO, Innio +15%, Liftoff +21%, Sunshine Silver +11% — strong IPO demand signals investor appetite. However, crypto weakness (BTC below $62K) and Saylor's Strategy paper loss ($11B) suggest capital rotating FROM speculative tech TO IPO opportunities.

factors.name: Real yields / Treasury curve stress
factors.summary: BOE Bailey warns of market stress and leverage risks; UK 10Y up 97bps (4.23% to 5.20%), US 10Y up 76bps (3.92% to 4.687%). Rising real yields compress NDX multiples; war-weary Treasury market losing patience per MarketWatch.

factors.name: Labor market resilience mixed signals
factors.summary: Long-term unemployment surging per 2026 data; Treas Sec Bessent hints at strong tomorrow's jobs report ('I wish job market had come out today') — suggests strong NFP may be priced in but also fuels Fed hawkish lean.

factors.name: AI capex spending thesis intact but questioned
factors.summary: AI adoption 'incredibly shallow' per Bloomberg; Wasted AI budgets at Nvidia, Uber, Microsoft noted as 'tokenmaxxing' reversed. Job market benefiting as human workers cheaper than AI. Cracks in hyperscaler AI ROI narrative.

factors.name: Price action: modest 4h and 24h gains already booked
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.31% in 4h, +0.64% in 24h — very modest moves despite mixed news. Suggests market in digestion phase; no major breakout, but also no major breakdown yet.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between competing forces: strong earnings misses in semiconductors (Broadcom, Micron), persistent Fed hawkish inflation messaging, and rising real yields are pressuring long-duration tech multiples and triggering rotation into value/financials (Dow +1.84%). Conversely, geopolitical de-risking (Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, Ukraine peace optimism), IPO appetite, and intact (if questioned) AI capex thesis provide some support. The market appears to be in a rebalancing phase: mega-cap tech valuations are being re-tested, and the narrative has shifted from 'all-in AI' to 'AI adoption shallow, capex efficiency under scrutiny.' Modest 4h/24h price action suggests consolidation ahead of Friday's NFP report, which will likely re-anchor Fed expectations.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) Friday 2026-06-05 — critical for Fed rate expectations; Bessent's hint suggests strong beat likely
FOMC meeting minutes and next policy decision (timing TBD but likely in June cycle)
Earnings from mega-cap tech: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA — forward guidance on capex and cloud spending
10-year Treasury yield trajectory — if continues above 4.70%, real yields remain headwind for NDX
Semiconductor supply chain updates (TSMC, ASML, Samsung) following Broadcom/Micron weakness
China-Taiwan tensions and AI chip export control announcements
screening.risk_warning: If NFP Friday comes in materially cooler than expected (sub-150K print), Fed pivot to easing could reverse the bearish real-yield pressure and reignite tech/growth rally. Conversely, if NFP accelerates inflation trajectory, Fed hawkish hold could extend NDX selloff. Mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL) with upside guidance and strong capex outlooks would also invalidate the bearish semiconductor/capex ROI narrative. Watch for single-name dealer gamma in NVDA, AAPL, TSLA — if those bounce hard, NDX follows.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is too mixed (conflicting inflation/Fed hawkishness vs. geopolitical relief) to warrant aggressive directional entry. Wait for NFP Friday or for one of the competing narratives to dominate; for now, consider small NEUTRAL hedge (straddle) or wait for NDX to test 30,200 support or break 30,700 resistance with conviction before entering. Avoid chasing into Friday's event risk.
